business_thesis: Keep the demo credible by tying every visible claim to deterministic data generation, static artifacts, local API behavior, and repeatable tests.
architecture_findings: The web surface reads prebuilt JSON by default and uses the local API only when present.
The optional LLM adapter seam does not participate in scoring or finding generation.
backend_schema_findings: Generated JSON keeps corpora, findings, patterns, dynamics, scorecards, and hard-negative evidence separated enough for focused frontend rendering.
The standard-library server avoids deployment dependency creep for the demo path.
security_findings: No API key is required for deterministic analysis or static browsing.
The protected local .env path remains outside planned write targets.
accuracy_eval_findings: The deterministic core, web serializer, build process, and server routes are covered by the project test run.
Model-backed chat is framed as explanatory rather than evidentiary.
scalability_findings: Static output can be hosted cheaply while heavier model interactions stay behind a local or future service boundary.
Future market builds should add fixture versioning before accepting uploaded corpora.
frontend_design_findings: Frontend edits are scoped to the static shell and do not alter analysis semantics.
recommendation_to_claude: Approve the professional demo pass after tests and browser checks stay green.
